openingHoursBusinessConfirmationText: Confirmed by phone call 7 weeks ago

reviews.text:
**A Quiet Gem: Le Saint Honoré on Rue de Carouge**

Le Saint Honoré on Geneva’s Rue de Carouge is a charming, unpretentious bakery that feels like a step back in time. The warm, buttery scent of fresh croissants greets you, and the almond croissant—crisp outside, soft and nutty within—is a standout. The apple pie is rustic and satisfying, with perfectly balanced dough. Open at all hours, it’s a haven for locals and night owls, with friendly staff who make you feel at home. Some batches can vary, and prices reflect Geneva’s 2025 economy, but the quality is over the top. A no-frills spot for excellent pastries and a dose of old-school warmth.

reviews.text:
Charming, bustling café in a lovely neighbourhood. Staff were friendly and helpfully explained things in English.

Great mix of savoury (like salmon quiche and croque monsieur) and sweet (pastries, eclairs) options—flavourful and rich. The crowd reflects its broad appeal: young and old, sweet tooths and savoury lovers alike.

Coffee was a quiet standout—subtly excellent and best appreciated after the meal.
reviews.name: Maria Marzuki

reviews.text: I have travelled the world extensively over 40 years. I ate at all restaurants ranging from pop & mom’s to Michelin three stars. This is my honest review of the bakery Le Saint Honoré in Geneva. This bakery feels like time stopped in a good way. Wide choices of traditional pastries. I tried the apple pie and the almond croissant which were both delicious (in particular the dough). The interior is a bit old fashioned but charming at the same time.

La Maison Paillasse est une adresse incontournable pour la qualité du pain, des pâtisseries ou des snacks d'une grande variété.

Mais ce qui rend ce qui rend chaque visite si spéciale, c'est l'accueil tout simplement incroyable. On se sent comme à la maison, grâce à une équipe d'une gentillesse et d'une chaleur inégalables. Le sourire et la bonne humeur sont toujours au rendez-vous.

Un grand merci à toute l'équipe de La Maison Paillasse pour leur service impeccable et leur sens de l'hospitalité. C'est ce qui fait la différence et qui me donne envie de revenir encore et encore. Une adresse à ne manquer sous aucun prétexte !

reviews.textTranslated:
A warm atmosphere and very pleasant staff. I was eagerly awaiting the opening of Maison Paillasse. After tasting their offerings, I highly recommend: the croque-monsieur, incredibly melty in a Nordic-style bread; the savory paillochoc with cheddar and chorizo ​​(absolutely amazing); the dark and white chocolate cookie; and my sweet favorite: the jam squares! Extremely indulgent, generously filled, and perfectly soft. I thoroughly enjoyed them.

I'll be back to try more of their products, but this first impression was very positive. Well done! 👏🏼
reviews.text:
Atmosphère chaleureuse et personnel très agréable. J’attendais l’ouverture de Maison Paillasse avec impatience. Après dégustation, je recommande vivement : le croque-monsieur, super fondant dans un pain de type nordique ; le paillochoc en version salée avec du cheddar et du chorizo (une dinguerie) ; le cookie chocolat noir et blanc ; et mon coup de cœur sucré : les carrés à la confiture ! Ultra gourmand, généreux en garniture et moelleux à souhait. Je me suis régalé.
J’y retournerai pour tester davantage de produits, mais cette première impression fut très positive. Bravo 👏🏼

reviews.textTranslated:
I was absolutely delighted! Pâtisserie Berger in Champel isn't just a pastry shop, it's a perfect lunch spot.

Their croissants are exactly how I like them: crispy on the outside, with a light and airy, buttery crumb inside. A real treat first thing in the morning!

The welcome is incredibly warm and professional. The staff is attentive, friendly, and offers helpful advice if you're unsure what to order.
Congratulations and a huge thank you to the entire team for their exceptional work!

PS: Don't hesitate to try their macarons and bread; they're also excellent. I highly recommend it.
reviews.text:
J'ai été absolument conquis! La Pâtisserie Berger à Champel, se n'est pas simplement une pâtisserie, mais une pose déjeuner

Leurs croissants sont exactement comme je les aime : croustillants à l'extérieur, avec une mie alvéolée et beurrée à l'intérieur. Un régal dès le matin !

l'accueil est extrêmement chaleureux et professionnel. Le personnel est à l'écoute, souriant et de bon conseil si vous hésitez.
Félicitations et un grand merci à toute l'équipe pour ce travail d'une qualité rare !
PS : N'hésitez pas à essayer leurs macarons et leur pain, ils sont également excellents.
Je recommande

reviews.text: A true French delight in the heart of Geneva! Boulangerie Martine on Rue Jean-Violette absolutely deserves a special mention for its outstanding viennoiseries. The croissants, pain au chocolat, and other pastries are incredibly fresh, with a soft texture and authentic flavor that stand out far above other Geneva bakeries, where viennoiseries are often dry and lackluster. Every bite is a pleasure, with perfectly flaky pastry and a taste reminiscent of the best Parisian bakeries. A must-try!
reviews.name: Yannick Deforel

reviews.text: Boulangerie Martine is a gem! Certainly there are many choices of boulangerie in Geneva - but Martine is truly exceptional. The patisseries, the desserts, the soups and the food are all fresh and tasty. Sit and eat & relax, or take away. But the real “secret” of this boulangerie is the owner, Alex. He’s present all the time, with a warm and helpful welcome for every customer. Visiting Martine will brighten your day - you’ll leave with delicious treats and a smile on your face. We’re so fortunate to have discovered Martine the day after we moved to the neighborhood.

reviews.text:
I purchased baguettes de tradition and a loaf of sourdough bread, as well as piece of Black Forest cake and red fruits millefeuille.

The bread was good, almost as good as what you get from French artisan bakeries. The desserts on the other hand were mediocre. The cake did not have enough sour cherries and was mainly composed of cream and chocolate shavings (and was needlessly sweet). The millefeuille was not flaky (soggy and not fresh) and the Chantilly cream used did not go well with it (crème patissière would have been a better alternative). To be fair though, I did my shopping around closure time. Kudos for serving the millefeuille flipped on its side which makes it very easy to cut (pro tip).

The service was very courteous and quick.
